__naming_note__: The doc_type name 'BILL_OF_LANDING' is a typo of the correct term 'BILL_OF_LADING' (lading = loading cargo, not landing). The schema name is preserved for backward compatibility, but the document this hint describes is the Bill of Lading (and the related Sea Waybill).
bill_of_landing_number: Extract ONLY the B/L No. (or Sea Waybill No.) — the main document identifier, usually in the upper right header. Labels: "BILL OF LADING No." / "SEA WAYBILL No." / "B/L No.". DO NOT confuse with three similar-looking IDs that often appear in the same document: (a) "Booking No." / "Booking Ref." / "Shipper's Ref." — booking reference; (b) "Service Contract Number" — carrier service contract; (c) "Carrier Reference" / "Tracking No". All look like multi-character IDs but are semantically different. Keep the number exactly as printed (letters/digits/spaces).
bill_of_landing_date: Extract the official issue date of the B/L from "Place and date of issue" / "Date of Issue of B/L". DO NOT confuse with: (a) "Shipped on Board Date" — the actual loading date, often days or weeks earlier; (b) "ISSUE DATE" inside the goods description block — that is typically the veterinary certificate issue date (next to "VET.CERT.:"). Add to bill_of_landing_date.
exporter: Take from the "SHIPPER" block. Keep the full name and address as one line. Add to exporter.
importer: Take from the "CONSIGNEE" block. Keep the full name and address as one line. Add to importer.
destination: Extract by labels "PORT OF DISCHARGE" or "PLACE OF DELIVERY". Priority: PORT OF DISCHARGE if PLACE OF DELIVERY is empty or absent (port-to-port shipping). If PLACE OF DELIVERY is present and differs from PORT OF DISCHARGE (multimodal shipping), use PLACE OF DELIVERY (final destination). Ignore placeholder values like "XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" — these mean "no data". Add to destination.
vessel: Find the "VESSEL AND VOYAGE NO." line (vessel name and voyage number, e.g., "MSC AMBRA - GJ519W", "LEXA MAERSK / 935N"). Both dash and slash separators are valid. Keep the full line and add to vessel.
container_no: Extract container number(s) in format 4 letters + 7 digits (e.g., "CRXU6954811", "PONU4832160"). The same block typically also contains: container type ("40' HIGH CUBE REEFER" / "40 REEF 9'6"), seal number, tare weight, marks and numbers, temperature — these belong in their own fields (when supported). If multiple containers, join with "; ". Add to container_no.
linear_seal: Extract linear seal number(s) by labels "Seal Number" / "SEAL NO." / "Shipper Seal" / "Container Seal" / "Carrier Seal". The seal number may appear ON ANY PAGE of a multi-page B/L, not only in the main container block — check all pages including the back. If multiple, join with "; ". Add to linear_seal.
veterinary_seal: Look for explicit veterinary seal labels ("Veterinary seal" / "Health seal"). Do not confuse with "Seal Number" / "Shipper Seal" (linear seal) or "VET.CERT." / "VETERINARY CERTIFICATE" (vet certificate number — that is a different document). If no explicit vet seal is shown, leave empty. Add the value to veterinary_seal.
packages: Extract number of packages by labels "No. of packages" / "Case(s)" / "Carton(s)" / "Packages" / "Bag(s)". If "Total Items" is shown, use the package count for the cargo, not the number of containers ("Carrier's Receipt: 1 container" is the container count, not the package count). If the description lists multiple package counts by size (e.g., "832 CARTONS + 48 CARTONS"), sum them and verify against the stated total (e.g., "880 CARTONS"). Add to packages.
net_weight: Extract net weight by "N.W." or "NET WEIGHT". Net weight is given INSIDE the goods description block (e.g., "N.W.: 27149.20KGS", "22,000.00 KGS NET WEIGHT"), NOT in a separate column. The right-side column labeled "Weight" or "Gross Cargo Weight" is the GROSS, not the net. Keep the number with unit as in the document. Add to net_weight.
gross_weight: Extract gross weight by "GROSS WEIGHT" / "GROSS CARGO WEIGHT". B/L gross weight is the TOTAL container+cargo weight (no "with pallets" / "without pallets" distinction in B/L — that distinction belongs to packing list/CMR). May be duplicated in both the right-side weight column and the description block — extract once. Add to gross_weight.
country_of_origin: B/L typically does NOT contain explicit country of origin — that is for CO and invoice. If "COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: ..." appears inside the goods description as a fallback, extract it. Otherwise leave empty. Do NOT infer from shipper or loading port.
name_product: Extract ONLY the commercial name from the goods description block. EXCLUDE: container info ("1X40' HC REEFER..."), package counts ("880 CARTONS"), weights ("22,000.00 KGS NET WEIGHT", "GROSS WEIGHT..."), sizes ("SIZE 6-9 LB..."), certificate numbers ("VETERINARY CERTIFICATE:"), commodity codes, freight terms ("FREIGHT PREPAID"), factory info, and any container/seal numbers. The commercial name typically starts with "FROZEN" / "FRESH" / "CHILLED" + species. Add to name_product.
latin_name: Latin species name in binomial format (Genus species), in parentheses (e.g., "(SALMO SALAR)", "(ONCORHYNCHUS MYKISS)"). If not, leave empty.
commodity_code: Extract HS/commodity code if present. Labels: "HS CODE" / "COMMODITY CODE" / "Tariff code". Format may be 6 digits with dot ("0304.81"), 10 digits without separators ("0303142000"), or 10 digits with spaces ("0303 14 2000"). The code is often DUPLICATED in the description — extract one unique value. May be entirely ABSENT (typical in Sea Waybills) — if so, leave empty.
size_product: If size/grade is present in the description (e.g., "3-4 LB", "80/120", "SIZE 6-9 LB"), extract and add to size_product. If not shown, leave empty.
